title: Secure, Performant, and Maintainable Python Delivery · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: Deliver a trustworthy local automation product for a community program with operator and learner documentation. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a production-shaped local automation capstone and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Turn stakeholder stories into explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, failures, acceptance checks, and staged implementation work.
Treat files, arguments, environment values, paths, and subprocess data as untrusted boundaries with least authority.
Keep secrets out of source and logs while validating configuration presence, provenance, and safe defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-basics cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Python Capstone Evidence evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Python Capstone Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Defend architecture, correctness, accessibility of terminal output, security, failure recovery, and future extensions with evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python release capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Python Capstone Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a production-shaped local automation capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A demonstration of the happy path is sufficient capstone evidence. This fails the Python Capstone Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: The Python Standard Library (Python 3.14.6) — Controls files, paths, serialization, command-line, testing, logging, processes, and standard tools.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend architecture, correctness, accessibility of terminal output, security, failure recovery, and future extensions with evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Add a evidence_python_capstone_evidence_1vfeeh function that computes or traces the changed case, asserts one meaningful invariant, and returns inspectable evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Python Capstone Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Python Capstone Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend architecture, correctness, accessibility of terminal output, security, failure recovery, and future extensions with evidence.
